title: Determine retail rates for automotive products and services
training_package_name: Automotive Retail, Service and Repair Training Package
competent_person_statement: A competent person in Determine retail rates for automotive products and services demonstrates consistent performance across all elements and performance criteria in a workplace context.

elements.title: Determine fixed costs

pcs.text: Fixed cost components are identified and classified into categories1.2 Past records and business requirements are used to estimate fixed costs for next period1.3 Fixed administrative and business operation overhead costs are calculated1.4 Proportion of fixed costs to be included in retail rates is determined
pcs.analysis_prompt: What valid, sufficient, authentic and current evidence shows the candidate can Fixed cost components are identified and classified into categories1.2 Past records and business requirements are used to estimate fixed costs for next period1.3 Fixed administrative and business operation overhead costs are calculated1.4 Proportion of fixed costs to be included in retail rates is determined?
pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Fixed cost components are identified and classified into categories1.2 Past records and business requirements are used to estimate fixed costs for next period1.3 Fixed administrative and business operation overhead costs are calculated1.4 Proportion of fixed costs to be included in retail rates is determined.

elements.analysis_focus: Analyse evidence against Element E1: Determine fixed costs

elements.title: Determine labour rate

pcs.text: Cost components of labour are identified and classified into categories2.2 Average charge per hour for each identified category is calculated2.3 Cost component of subcontractors used on jobs is determined and included in calculations for relevant jobs2.4 Hourly labour rates are calculated according to workplace procedures
pcs.analysis_prompt: What valid, sufficient, authentic and current evidence shows the candidate can Cost components of labour are identified and classified into categories2.2 Average charge per hour for each identified category is calculated2.3 Cost component of subcontractors used on jobs is determined and included in calculations for relevant jobs2.4 Hourly labour rates are calculated according to workplace procedures?
pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Cost components of labour are identified and classified into categories2.2 Average charge per hour for each identified category is calculated2.3 Cost component of subcontractors used on jobs is determined and included in calculations for relevant jobs2.4 Hourly labour rates are calculated according to workplace procedures.

elements.analysis_focus: Analyse evidence against Element E2: Determine labour rate

elements.title: Determine variable costs

pcs.text: Variable costs are identified and calculated using data obtained from accounting records or supplier information3.2 Required materials are estimated and recorded
pcs.analysis_prompt: What valid, sufficient, authentic and current evidence shows the candidate can Variable costs are identified and calculated using data obtained from accounting records or supplier information3.2 Required materials are estimated and recorded?
pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Variable costs are identified and calculated using data obtained from accounting records or supplier information3.2 Required materials are estimated and recorded.

elements.analysis_focus: Analyse evidence against Element E3: Determine variable costs

elements.title: Determine retail price of products and services

pcs.text: Previous sales figures and trends in turnover volume are reviewed4.2 Competitors’ price for similar products or services is researched and reflected in final retail rate4.3 Retail prices, margins and mark-ups for each product or service category are finalised according to workplace procedures
pcs.analysis_prompt: What valid, sufficient, authentic and current evidence shows the candidate can Previous sales figures and trends in turnover volume are reviewed4.2 Competitors’ price for similar products or services is researched and reflected in final retail rate4.3 Retail prices, margins and mark-ups for each product or service category are finalised according to workplace procedures?
pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Previous sales figures and trends in turnover volume are reviewed4.2 Competitors’ price for similar products or services is researched and reflected in final retail rate4.3 Retail prices, margins and mark-ups for each product or service category are finalised according to workplace procedures.

elements.analysis_focus: Analyse evidence against Element E4: Determine retail price of products and services
